concerns.text: The plan explicitly states 'NO VERIFIED SOURCE' for Open X-Embodiment/BridgeData V2 and proposes a fallback to 'synthetic MuJoCo trajectory generator' or 'mock' data. While the spec (FR-001) mandates a stratified subset of real robotics datasets, the plan admits the cited datasets are not verified in the provided list. Training a world model on synthetic data generated by the same simulator (MuJoCo) used for evaluation creates a circular validation loop that cannot answer the research question regarding 'open-world' generalization or sample efficiency on real-world distribution shifts. The dataset strategy is inappropriate for the stated hypothesis.

The plan states the study will 'train both models on a stratified subset of robotics manipulation data' but the research.md admits the specific datasets (Open X/BridgeData) are unverified and fallbacks to synthetic data. If the training data is synthetic (generated by the same MuJoCo engine used for testing), the 'Long-Horizon Success Rate' and 'Physics Consistency Score' metrics will be trivially satisfied or biased, failing to validate the 'Hybrid Multi-Scale Temporal Memory' against real-world complexity. The dataset choice (or lack thereof) invalidates the comparative study.
